flashcards.front: What is the first step in the ML process?
flashcards.back: Problem Definition — understand the business context and formulate the problem as an ML task (classification, regression, clustering, etc.).

flashcards.front: Which step in the ML process takes the most time?
flashcards.back: Data Preparation and Exploration — typically 60-80% of total project time (cleaning, EDA, feature engineering, selection).

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the validation set?
flashcards.back: To tune hyperparameters and evaluate model performance during training, without touching the test set.
When the statistical relationship between input features and target variable changes over time, causing model accuracy to degrade.

flashcards.front: Name the typical data split ratios.

flashcards.back: Creating new input features from existing data using domain knowledge (e.g., extracting day-of-week from a date column).
